messages.text: There is nothing better than gaming in a good mood.
When you are down, even the most moving RPG scenes fail to reach you. But when you are happy, even a total party wipe that ruins hours of progress only makes you laugh.
That is why today — even sitting alone in the classroom corner playing a mobile social game — I am completely absorbed.
I reflect on yesterday as I tap through quests. Tendou Karen — the school idol — invited me to join the Game Club.
A dream event dropped into my gloomy school life out of nowhere. My idol approached me herself. I might soon have friends who love games.
For the first time, I genuinely anticipate going to school. Although a slight anxiety lingers — what if this is all a dream?
Yesterday, after the game store, we sat on a park bench. Tendou explained the club while sipping from a small water bottle.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: I founded the club, but I have not done any public recruitment. No posters. No announcements.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Instead, each member personally finds people with real love for games. It is slow and inefficient. We might miss many good players.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: But letting in the wrong people could destroy everything. I want to build a solid foundation first. Quality over speed.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Anyway — tomorrow! Keep your after-school time free! See you then, Amano-kun!
I stared at the ceiling. Was I really chosen by her? It had nothing to do with romance. Still — something I could only fantasize about now felt real.
That was the first day in my life I truly looked forward to coming to school. Even now I replay the memory again and again as I clear quests.
Then a rescue request notification pops up. Another player needs help beating a limited-time event boss.
It is from MONO — a friend I added when I first started this game. We have never exchanged a single message, yet I feel a strange bond.
I reach out to tap the accept button. But then —

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Ah, found you, Amano-kun!
She enters the classroom with a bright voice. The room goes silent. An impossible figure in our ordinary space.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Hmm? What are you busy with?

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Ah, just... passing time...

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Oh, a casual social game. I am surprised. I did not expect you to play such boring things.
My face goes hot. Does she despise casual games? I flip the phone face-down on my desk. I avoid meeting her eyes.

messages.name: Karen
messages.text: Anyway, about today. Let us meet at the library after school. Can you come right after cleaning duty?

messages.name: Keita
messages.text: Yes. I think... that should be fine.
She leaves with a wave. I pick up my phone and check the screen. Rescue Failed. A wave of guilt washes over me.
I sacrificed helping a friend for a casual conversation. The club must be my priority now — but I cannot focus on anything.
